title: Permit bundling multiple UX sprints onto one branch/PR (deviation from 1:1 sprint-branch mapping)
decision: Allow Sprint 19 (System Tray & Notification Routing) and Sprint 20 (Session Groups & Templates) to be developed on a single work branch (work/<ts>/sprint-19-20-tray-and-organization), promoted through one stage/test branch, and merged via one pull request. This is a conscious, user-authorized deviation from claude.git-hygiene.md's normative 1:1 sprint-to-work-branch mapping. git-hygiene.md is amended to permit this for closely related, non-daemon UX sprints.

context.task_description: Starting the final two Flow II sprints. Both are pure renderer/main-process UX work and neither touches the daemon. The user's established workflow bundles independent UX sprints to save promotion overhead, while daemon-touching sprints (17, 18) each get their own PR.
assumptions: Sprints 19 and 20 are independent UX features that do not touch the daemon protocol
Per-sprint traceability is preserved via sprint files, commit scoping, and this decision record
Auditability requirement of git-hygiene (retain branches) is unaffected

options.label: Two PRs (strict 1:1 compliance)
options.description: Sprint 19 and Sprint 20 each get their own work -> stage/test -> main path and separate PR. Fully compliant with git-hygiene 1:1 mapping; no spec change needed.

options.label: One bundled PR (logged deviation + spec amendment)
options.description: Bundle 19+20 on one branch/PR. Record this decision and amend git-hygiene.md with an allowed-exception clause so spec and practice agree.

rationale: The user explicitly chose to bundle these two UX sprints after the conflict with the normative 1:1 mapping was surfaced. Both sprints are independent, renderer-heavy, and daemon-free, so the per-sprint isolation the rule protects (avoiding entanglement and preserving traceability) is satisfied by sprint files and scoped commits. Amending the spec keeps governance honest rather than leaving an undocumented violation.

risk.rollback: Future sprints revert to strict 1:1 mapping; the exception clause can be removed from git-hygiene.md. Bundled branch history remains in git for audit.

outcome.notes: Exception scoped to closely related, non-daemon UX sprints; daemon-touching sprints still map 1:1 to branches/PRs.
